last_assistant_message: Deployed. Check https://vnzla-oil.vercel.app — the fix loads Chart.js and Leaflet via direct DOM script injection in the correct order (Chart.js first, then Leaflet, then dashboard JS), so the charts and tables should now render.

Deployed. The approach is now completely different — no React hydration of the dashboard at all:

- **`/` redirects to `/dashboard.html`** — the exact same static HTML file that works locally
- **Charts, tables, map** all render via the original inline `<script>` — no Next.js interference
- **News ribbon** — scrolling ticker below the topbar with live headlines from `/api/news` (Google News RSS for "venezuela oil production")
- **Live prices** — fetches `/api/prices` on load and updates the Brent/WTI ticker values

Check https://vnzla-oil.vercel.app now — should show all charts and data since it's the same HTML that works on your local machine.

Fixed. The API returns `{items: [...]}` but the JS was expecting a bare array. Now it reads `data.items`. The news ribbon should scroll with headlines like "US broadly authorizes transactions with Venezuela's oil company PDVSA" from Reuters, AP News, Axios, etc.

Deployed. Here's what changed:

**Mobile UI** — three breakpoints:
- **900px**: sidebar collapses
- **768px**: KPI grid → 2 columns, all chart rows → single column, smaller chart heights, scenario descriptions hidden, tighter table cells
- **480px**: further compressed KPIs, topbar simplified to just brand, modal goes 95vw

**News ribbon** — slowed from 60s to 140s (more than 2x slower, easy to read). Pauses on hover.

**Multi-source news** — now queries 4 Google News RSS feeds in parallel:
1. Venezuela oil production + sanctions
2. PDVSA + Chevron + Venezuela
3. Brent crude + Hormuz
4. OPEC + oil market

Results are deduped, sorted by recency, top 15 articles. Much fresher and broader coverage.

**X posts ready** — `/api/x` endpoint built and wired into the ribbon. When you add a `TWITTER_BEARER_TOKEN` env var in Vercel, it'll fetch recent tweets about Venezuela oil/PDVSA and show them with a blue "X" tag in the ribbon (higher-speed alpha). To set it up:

```
vercel env add TWITTER_BEARER_TOKEN
```

Until then, the ribbon runs on news RSS only — no errors, graceful fallback.
Slowed to 300s (5 minutes for a full cycle). Should feel like a slow, readable crawl now.

Done. 

**News ribbon** — now `position:fixed` at the top of the viewport, stays visible as you scroll through the entire dashboard.

**Footer** — updated with categorized sources and update cadence:
- Prices: every 5 min (Yahoo Finance live)
- News: every 30 min (Google News RSS + X API)
- Production: monthly (OPEC/CEIC)
- Dynamic "Last loaded" timestamp that updates on each page visit

Done. The layout is now:

```
VNZLA OIL | Venezuela Crude Oil Production Intelligence | 2026 Guidance Dashboard
LIVE  [====== scrolling news ribbon fills the rest of the bar ======]
BRENT MAY $118.35 +5.0% | BRENT JUN $108.00 ...
```

The nav bar with LIVE + ribbon sits between the topbar and the ticker, stays fixed while you scroll since it's outside the `.main` scroll container.

Deployed. The mobile layout now:

**768px and below** (phones):
- Topbar collapses to just "VNZLA OIL" brand + timestamp
- Ticker becomes horizontally scrollable (swipe)
- KPIs → 2 columns
- All chart rows → single column (stacked)
- Charts shorter (180/160px)
- Scenarios → single column, descriptions hidden
- Tables → horizontal scroll with touch momentum
- Map → 280px height
- Everything gets tighter padding/font sizes
- `overflow-x:hidden` on body prevents any horizontal bleed

**390px** (small phones like iPhone SE):
- Even smaller KPIs and charts
- Scenario cards hidden entirely
- Map → 220px

Much fresher — **13 minutes old** for the newest article, vs 2+ hours before. 20 articles total from Bloomberg, OilPrice, FXEmpire, WION, and Google News aggregation.

The improvement:
- **Direct publisher RSS feeds** (Bloomberg, OilPrice, CNBC) give near-real-time articles
- **Google News `when:1d`** filters to last 24h only
- **10-minute cache** instead of 30 minutes
- **Oil keyword filter** ensures non-oil articles from general feeds get dropped

Deployed. Two fixes:

**News ribbon on mobile** — the `.nav` now has `overflow:hidden` + `position:relative` so the ribbon's absolute-positioned scrolling inner div works within the nav bar height on mobile.

**Chart filters** — the panel header uses `flex-wrap:nowrap` + `overflow-x:auto` instead of `flex-wrap:wrap`. All controls (Monthly/Quarterly/Annual | 1Y/3Y/5Y/Max | Projections) stay in one horizontal row. If the screen is too narrow, you can swipe to see the rest — no awkward second line. Title also shortens to "Prod & Scenarios" on mobile.

Deployed. Two things fixed:

**Mobile news ribbon** — replaced the fixed 300s CSS animation with JS-driven adaptive speed. It now calculates content width vs container width and sets speed at 50px/sec, so on a phone screen (narrow container) the ribbon moves at a readable pace instead of crawling invisibly.

**EN/ES language toggle** — click `EN / ES` in the top-right corner of the topbar. Switches ~60 strings:
- KPI labels (Producción, Deuda Externa, etc.)
- All panel titles (Producción Histórica y Escenarios 2026, etc.)
- Sidebar sections (Cronología de Sanciones, Los Seis Grandes, etc.)
- Scenario cards (Pesimista, Base, Optimista)
- Context headings
- Chart filter labels
- Footer link

Default is English. The data values (numbers, company names, ticker prices) stay as-is since they're universal.

Done.

**Table dividers** — vertical borders between every column (both headers and data cells), last column excluded. Makes it much easier to track rows across columns on mobile when scrolling horizontally.

**i18n removed** — language toggle, all `data-i18n` attributes, translation dictionary, and toggle functions all stripped. Clean slate.

Done. Desktop table looks clean again (no dividers). On mobile, each column now has a fixed pixel width (`table-layout:fixed`) so as you scroll horizontally, the columns stay perfectly aligned — Company 100px, Origin 75px, OFAC 90px, Prod 65px, Guidance 90px, Share 60px, Key JVs 130px. Text that overflows gets ellipsis-truncated.

The fix: set a hard total table width of 610px with `table-layout:fixed` and `max-width:0` on every cell. This forces the browser to respect the exact column widths instead of auto-expanding cells based on content. The share bar is also constrained to 30-40px so it can't push the column wider. All 7 columns should now align perfectly as you scroll.

Dashboard is live at **https://vnzla-oil.vercel.app** with all the GL-52 and NABEP corrections. Here's what updated:

- **NABEP**: excluded → **active at ~55K bpd** under GL-52 (Petrozamora, Junín Sur, Petrocedeño upgrader)
- **GL-52** added to sidebar timeline (Mar 18) + GL-46B/48A/49A (Mar 13)
- **Production baseline**: 903K → 958K (adds NABEP's 55K)
- **KPI**: operators now shows "7+" with GL-52 reference
- **Ticker**: reflects GL-52 opening to all US entities
- **Country bar**: US production 242K → 297K (Chevron + NABEP)
- **JV chart**: Petrozamora now shows NABEP as operator
- **Map markers**: Petrozamora + Petrocedeño updated to GL-52 active
- **Context section**: mentions GL-52 broadening access beyond Big Six

Good call checking the OFAC page directly — GL-52 was a major miss. That March 18 license fundamentally changes the landscape.

Done. The map now has **9 oil spill markers** (red circles with glow border) alongside the infrastructure:

**Spills mapped:**
- **LOL-3 Pipeline, Gulf of Coro** (Jan 4, 2025) — ~90,000 bbl, 70th spill in this area since 2019
- **Amuay Refinery overflow** (Late Jan 2025)
- **Lake Maracaibo chronic leaks** (ongoing) — ~1,000 bbl/day, 13,000 km² estuary
- **LOL Pipeline, Mitare Beach** (Feb 5, 2025) — >150K bbl cumulative, 7,000 fishing families affected
- **Monagas–José Pipeline** (Late Feb 2025) — >24,000 bbl, media blocked by National Guard
- **J-20 Station, Morichal** (~Jan 15, 2025) — up to ~50,000 bbl
- **El Palito / Golfo Triste** (Mar 2025) — 225 km² oil slick, Morrocoy National Park
- **LOL Pipeline, Carirubana** (Mar 25, 2026) — near Cardón refinery
- **LOL Pipeline, Miranda** (Mar 26, 2026) — 2nd failure within 24h

Click any red marker for full details: cause, volume, status, environmental impact, and source. Use the **Spills** filter tab to isolate just the spill markers.
